passages.title: Sunset for the Oil Business
passages.source_html:
Sunset for the Oil Business 


The world is about to run out of oil. Or perhaps not. It depends whom you believe… 


A 


Members of the Department Analysis Centre (ODAC) recently met in London and presented technical data that support their grim forecast that the world is perilously close to running out of oil. Leading lights of this moment, including the geologists Colin Campbell, rejected rival views presented by American geological survey and the international energy agency that contradicted their findings. Dr Campbell even decried the amazing display of ignorance, denial and obfuscation by government, industry and academics on this topic. 


B 


So is the oil really running out? The answer is easy: Yes. Nobody seriously disputes the notion that oil is, for all practical purposes, a non-renewable resource that will run out someday, be that years or decades away. The harder question is determining when precisely oil will begin to get scarce. And answering that question involves scaling Hubbert's peak. 


C 


M. King Hubbert, a Shell geologist of legendary status among depletion experts, forecast in 1956 that oil production in the United States would peak in the early 1970s and then slowly decline, in something resembling a bell-shaped curve. At the time, his forecast was controversial, and many rubbished it. After 1970, however, empirical evidence proved him correct: oil production in America did indeed peak and has been in decline ever since.


D 


Dr Hubbert's analysis drew on the observation that oil production in a new area typically rises quickly at first, as the easiest and cheapest reserves are tapped. Over time, reservoirs age and go into decline, and so lifting oil becomes more expensive. Oil from that area then becomes less competitive in relation to other fuels, or to oil from other areas. As a result, production slows down and usually tapers off and declines. That, he argued, made for a bell-shaped curve. 


E 


His successful prediction has emboldened a new generation of geologists to apply his methodology on a global scale. Chief among them are the experts at ODAC, who worry that the global peak in production will come in the next decade. Dr Campbell used to argue that the peak should have come already; he now thinks it is just around the corner. A heavyweight has now joined this gloomy chorus. Kenneth Deffeyes of Princeton University argues in a lively new book (‘The View from Hubbert's Peak’) that global oil production could peak as soon as 2004. 


F 


That sharply contradicts mainstream thinking. America’s Geological Survey prepared an exhaustive study of oil depletion last year (in part to rebut Dr Campbell’s arguments) that put the peak of production some decades off. The IEA has just weighed in with its new ‘World Energy Outlook’, which foresees enough oil to comfortably meet the demand to 2020 from remaining reserves. René Dahan, one of ExxonMobil’s top managers, goes further: with an assurance characteristic of the world’s largest energy company, he insists that the world will be awash in oil for another 70 years. 


G 


Who is right? In making sense of these wildly opposing views, it is useful to look back at the pitiful history of oil forecasting. Doomsters have been predicting dry wells since the 1970s, but so far the oil is still gushing. Nearly all the predictions for 2000 made after the 1970s oil shocks were far too pessimistic. America’s Department of Energy thought that oil would reach $150 a barrel (at 2000 prices); even Exxon predicted a price of $100. 


H 


Michael Lynch of DRI-WEFA, an economic consultancy, is one of the few oil forecasters who has got things generally right. In a new paper, Dr Lynch analyses those historical forecasts. He finds evidence of both bias and recurring errors, which suggests that methodological mistakes (rather than just poor data) were the problem. In particular, he faults forecasters who used Hubbert-style analysis for relying on fixed estimates of how much ‘ultimately recoverable’ oil there really is below ground, in the industry’s jargon: that figure, he insists, is actually a dynamic one, as improvements in infrastructure, knowledge and technology raise the amount of oil which is recoverable. 


I 


That points to what will probably determine whether the pessimists or the optimists are right: technological innovation. The first camp tends to be dismissive of claims of forthcoming technological revolutions in such areas as deep-water drilling and enhanced recovery. Dr Deffeyes captures this end-of-technology mindset well. He argues that because the industry has already spent billions on technology development, it makes it difficult to ask today for new technology, as most of the wheels have already been invented. 


J 


Yet techno-optimists argue that the technological revolution in oil has only just begun. Average recovery rates (how much of the known oil in a reservoir can actually be brought to the surface) are still only around 30-35%. Industry optimists believe that new techniques on the drawing board today could lift that figure to 50-60% within a decade. 


K 


Given the industry’s astonishing track record of innovation, it may be foolish to bet against it. That is the result of adversity: the nationalisations of the 1970s forced Big Oil to develop reserves in expensive, inaccessible places such as the North Sea and Alaska, undermining Dr Hubbert’s assumption that cheap reserves are developed first. The resulting upstream investments have driven down the cost of finding and developing wells over the last two decades from over $20 a barrel to around $6 a barrel. The cost of producing oil has fallen by half, to under $4 a barrel. 


L 


Such miracles will not come cheap, however, since much of the world’s oil is now produced in ageing fields that are rapidly declining. The IEA concludes that global oil production need not peak in the next two decades if the necessary investments are made. So how much is necessary? If oil companies are to replace the output lost at those ageing fields and meet the world’s ever-rising demand for oil, the agency reckons they must invest $1 trillion in non-OPEC countries over the next decade alone. That’s quite a figure.

groups.title: Questions 27-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 27-31 


Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in passage? In boxes 27-31 on your answer sheet, write YES if the statement agrees with the information, NO if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.


27

Hubbert has a high-profile reputation amongst ODAC members.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：M. King Hubbert, a Shell geologist of legendary status among depletion experts, forecast in 1956 that oil production in the United States would peak in the early 1970s and then slowly decline, in something resembling a bell-shaped curve.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问Hubbert在ODAC成员中是否有很高的知名度。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"Hubbert"和"ODAC"定位答案。在C段中找到"Hubbert"的相关描述。

解题思路：原文中他是一个“geologist of legendary status”，在资源枯竭研究领域堪称传奇人物，对应题目信息“high-profile reputation”。题目与原文是同意表达，所以答案为Yes。

28

Oil is likely to last longer than some other energy sources.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：Oil from that area then becomes less competitive in relation to other fuels, or to oil from other areas.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是“石油耗尽可能比其他能源耗尽要晚”是否与作者的观点一致。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是“Oil”、“last longer than”、“other energy sources”。需要在文章中寻找关于石油和其他能源持续时间比较的信息。

解题思路：仔细阅读全文，寻找关于石油和其他能源持续时间比较的句子。在D段中，这里并没有提及题目中所说的“last longer than some other energy sources”，只是说石油作为能源不再有竞争力而已。题目信息在原文基础上无法判断，所以答案为Not Given。


29

The majority of geologists believe that oil with start to run out sometime this decade.

正确答案： B. NO

原文答案句：That sharply contradicts mainstream thinking. America’s Geological Survey prepared an exhaustive study of oil depletion last year (in part to rebut Dr Campbell’s arguments) that put the peak of production some decades off. 

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问“大多数地质学家认为石油将在这十年内开始耗尽”，即询问主流地质学家的观点是否认为石油将在这十年内开始减少。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的“majority of geologists”和“oil will start to run out sometime this decade”定位到F段，该段提到主流观点认为石油生产高峰将在几十年后到来，与题目中“这十年”的说法矛盾。

解题思路：F段明确指出主流地质学家的观点（包括美国地质调查局和国际能源机构）认为石油生产高峰将在几十年后到来，这与题目中“本十年”的说法相反。因此，题目中的说法与主流地质学家的观点相矛盾，应选择B（No）。


30

Over 50 percent of the oil we know about is currently being recovered.

正确答案： B. NO

原文答案句：Average recovery rates (how much of the known oil in a reservoir can actually be brought to the surface) are still only around 30-35%. Industry optimists believe that new techniques on the drawing board today could lift that figure to 50-60%within a decade

对应段落：J段  

审题：题目问“我们开采了超过50%的已知现存的石油”，即询问已知现存石油开采率是否超过50%。  

定位关键信息：题目中的"Over 50 percent"和"currently being recovered"对应J段中的"Average recovery rates"和"are still only around 30-35%"。  

解题思路：J段明确指出石油的平均开采率仅为30-35%，远低于题目中的"Over 50 percent"，50%是在未来人们可能达到的开采率，而不是现在。因此题目说法与原文矛盾，答案为No。


31

History has shown that some of Hubbert’s principles were mistaken.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：That is the result of adversity: the nationalisations of the 1970s forced Big Oil to develop reserves in expensive, inaccessible places such as the North Sea and Alaska, undermining Dr Hubbert’s assumption that cheap reserves are developed first.

对应段落：K段

审题：题目问的是“历史表明部分休伯特理论是错误的”，需要判断作者是否同意这个观点。

定位关键信息：使用 "Hubbert’s principles", "mistaken", "history has shown" 作为关键词，定位文中评价哈伯特理论准确性的句子。

解题思路：段落K中，作者明确指出，20世纪70年代的国有化政策迫使大石油公司去开发如北海和阿拉斯加这样成本高昂、难以进入的油田，这一历史事实“削弱了哈伯特的假设，即便宜的储量会先被开发”。原文用“undermining”（削弱）这个词，清晰地表明作者认为历史发展证明了哈伯特的这一特定原则（假设）是不正确的。这与题目陈述完全一致，因此答案为YES。


Questions 32-35


Complete the notes below. Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 32-35 on your answer sheet.


32

Many people believed Hubbert’s theory was 32 when it was originally presented.

正确答案： 32 controversial##Controversial
Question 32

原文答案句：At the time, his forecast was controversial, and many rubbished it. 

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问的是当Hubbert的理论最初提出时，许多人认为它是什么样的。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的"Hubbert’s theory"和"originally presented"可以定位到C段。

解题思路：根据原文中C段的内容，其中提到"his forecast was controversial, and many rubbished it"，这句话表明Hubbert的理论最初提出时受到了质疑和争议。因此答案为"controversial"。


33

(1) When an oilfield is 33 , it is easy to observe the increase of oil production.

正确答案： 33 new##New##tapped##Tapped
Question 33

原文答案句：Oil production in a new area typically rises quickly at first, as the easiest and cheapest reserves are tapped.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是当油田处于什么状态时，容易发现石油产量增加。

定位关键信息：根据题干中的"oilfield"和"oil production"定位到D段，该段描述了新油田的石油生产特点。

解题思路：原文提到新油田的石油产量初期通常增长迅猛，因为此时开采的是最容易获取、成本最低的油田。。这里的"tapped"表示油田被开采或处于新开发的状态。因此答案为tapped或new。


34

(2) The recovery of the oil gets more 34 as the reservoir gets older.

正确答案： 34 expensive##Expensive
Question 34

原文答案句：Over time, reservoirs age and go into decline, and so lifting oil becomes more expensive.  

对应段落：D段  

审题：题目问的是随着油藏老化，油开采会变得更什么。  

定位关键信息：利用题干中的关键词"recovery of the oil"和"reservoir gets older"定位到D段，找到描述油藏老化过程的句子。  

解题思路：原文中提到"reservoirs age and go into decline"（油藏老化并开始进入衰退期），紧接着说明"lifting oil becomes more expensive"（开采石油变得更昂贵）。题干中的"recovery of the oil"对应原文的"lifting oil"，"gets more"对应"becomes more"，因此答案为"expensive"。


35

(3) The oilfield can’t be as 35 as other areas.

正确答案： 35 competitive##Competitive
Question 35

原文答案句：Oil from that area then becomes less competitive in relation to other fuels, or to oil from other areas.  

对应段落：D段  

审题：题目问的是此时的油田无法像其他油田那样怎么样。  

定位关键信息：利用题干中的关键词"competitive"和"other areas"定位到D段后半部分，找到关于油田竞争力下降的描述。  

解题思路：原文中提到随着油田老化，开采成本增加，导致该油田的石油相对于其他燃料或其他油田，竞争力下降。题干中的"35"与原文中"less competitive"的描述对应，因此答案为competitive。


Questions 36-40


Look at the following statements (questions 36-40) and the list of people below. Match each statement with the correct person, A-E. Write the correct letter, A-E in boxes 36-40 on your answer sheet. 


NB You may use any letter more than once.


List of people 

A Colin Campell 

B M. King Hubbert 

C Kenneth Deffeyes 

D Rene Dahan 

E Michael Lynch 


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
36

36 has found fault in the geological research procedure. 
✅ 正确答案：E

原文答案句：In particular, he faults forecasters who used Hubbert-style analysis for relying on fixed estimates of how much ‘ultimately recoverable’ oil there really is below ground, in the industry’s jargon: that figure, he insists, is actually a dynamic one, as improvements in infrastructure, knowledge and technology raise the amount of oil which is recoverable.

对应段落：H段

审题：题目问的是“发现地质研究方法的问题”的人是谁。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词“has found fault in the geological research procedure”可以定位到H段，该段明确提到了“he faults forecasters who used Hubbert-style analysis for relying on fixed estimates”，这里的“he”指的是Michael Lynch，他对地质研究方法提出了批评。

解题思路：H段中的“he”指的是Michael Lynch，他批评了使用Hubbert风格分析的预测者，因为这些人把地下可开采石油量视为一个定量，而Michael Lynch认为这个数字实际上是动态的，因为基础设施、知识和技术的改进会增加可开采的石油量。因此，答案为E。
37

37 has provided the longest-range forecast regarding oil supply.
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：René Dahan, one of ExxonMobil’s top managers, goes further: with an assurance characteristic of the world’s largest energy company, he insists that the world will be awash in oil for another 70 years.  

对应段落：F段  

审题：题目问的是“谁提供了周期最长的石油供应预测。”  

定位关键信息：通过题目的关键词“longest-range forecast regarding oil supply”定位到F段，找到René Dahan的预测内容。  

解题思路：F段提到René Dahan是埃克森美孚的高级管理人员，他预测“世界将在未来70年内石油供应充足”，这是所有预测中时间跨度最大的，因此答案为D。
38

38 has convinced others that oil production will follow a particular model. 
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：His successful prediction has emboldened a new generation of geologists to apply his methodology on a global scale.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问的是谁让别人相信石油产量会遵循一个特定的模型。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"oil production will follow a particular model"定位到C段，该段提到Hubbert成功预测了美国石油产量会遵循一个特定的模型（bell-shaped curve），并因此激励了新一代地质学家将他的方法应用于全球。

解题思路：原文中C段提到Hubbert此前成功预测美国石油产量会遵循一个特定的模型，促使新一代地质学家将他的模型应用于全球。题目问的是谁让别人相信石油产量会遵循一个特定的模型，因此答案为Hubbert，对应选项B。
39

39 has accused fellow scientists of refusing to see the truth. 
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：Dr Campbell even decried the amazing display of ignorance, denial and obfuscation by government, industry and academics on this topic.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问的是“谁指责了同领域的科学家拒绝正视真相”。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词“accused fellow scientists of refusing to see the truth”可以定位到A段中“Dr Campbell even decried the amazing display of ignorance, denial and obfuscation by government, industry and academics on this topic.”这句话。

解题思路：原文中的“decried the amazing display of ignorance, denial and obfuscation”与题目中的“accused fellow scientists of refusing to see the truth”意思相符，坎贝尔医生痛斥政府、工业界和学术界在这一议题上展现出的无知、否认和混淆视听。因此答案为A。
40

40 has expressed doubt over whether improved methods of extracting oil are possible.
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：Dr Deffeyes captures this end-of-technology mindset well. He argues that because the industry has already spent billions on technology development, it makes it difficult to ask today for new technology, as most of the wheels have already been invented. 

对应段落：I  

审题：题目问的是“谁对是否可以改进石油开采方法表示怀疑”。  

定位关键信息：根据题目的关键词“improved methods of extracting oil”（改进的石油开采方法），在文章中定位到段落I，该段落讨论了对技术革新的不同看法。  

解题思路：段落I中，作者描述了悲观派对即将到来的技术革命持轻蔑态度。Deffeyes认为，由于行业已在技术研发上投入数十亿美元，如今再要求开发新技术已难如登天，因为“大多数轮子都已经被发明出来了”。这种认为重大技术创新已接近穷尽的观点，表达了对未来大幅改进采油方法的怀疑。因此，提出此观点的人是Kenneth Deffeyes，对应选项C。
